questions.question: Explain how cookies work in Java Servlets. Describe creating, reading, and deleting cookies with code examples.
keyPoints: Create: new Cookie(name, value), response.addCookie()
Read: request.getCookies() returns Cookie array, iterate to find
Delete: setMaxAge(0), add same-name cookie to response
Cookie properties: name, value, maxAge, path, domain
Code examples for each operation

questions.question: What are the advantages and limitations of using cookies for session tracking?
keyPoints: Advantages: automatic transmission, persistent storage, lightweight
Limitations: 4KB size limit, can be disabled, plain text storage
Security: use Secure and HttpOnly flags
Compare with server-side sessions for sensitive data
Best practices for cookie usage

questions.question: Write a servlet that uses cookies to remember a user's name and display a personalized greeting on their next visit.
keyPoints: Check for existing cookie with user name
If found: display personalized greeting
If not found: show form to enter name
On form submit: create cookie with name, set maxAge
Handle cookie arrays properly with null check

questions.question: Differentiate between session cookies and persistent cookies. Explain the role of setMaxAge().
keyPoints: Session cookies: no maxAge or negative, deleted when browser closes
Persistent cookies: positive maxAge, stored on disk
setMaxAge(seconds): controls cookie lifetime
setMaxAge(0): deletes the cookie
Use cases for each type
